$schema_note: Comp-filter heuristics for Phase 4A (ARV Intelligence). Bible v3 §9.2 mandates "reject distressed/cash/LLC sales," but RentCast doesn't expose buyer entity. These knobs approximate "renovated retail comps" via $/sqft floor relative to ZIP median + recency + similarity gates.
source: Bible v3 §9.2 + Crawler Roadmap §K (filters) + Phase 4A working assumption

distressed_proxy._note: Without buyer-entity data from RentCast, drop comps whose $/sqft is below this fraction of the ZIP median. Catches obvious cash/distressed flips that didn't see retail buyers.

bimodal_detection._note: When sorted $/sqft for surviving comps has a gap >= gap_threshold between adjacent values AND both sides have >= min_cluster_size, treat as bimodal. Upper cluster = renovated retail (kept when condition_target ∈ {good, renovated}); lower cluster = as-is wholesale flips (kept when condition_target ∈ {as_is, fair, poor}). Per-ZIP-prefix override; default applies elsewhere. SA threshold tighter because the bimodal gap there is smaller than Detroit/Memphis.

cross_method_disagreement._note: When BOTH the bimodal upper-cluster method AND the uplift model produce a renovated-ARV value, flag if they disagree by more than this fraction. Audit-logged as uncertain; confidence downgraded one step.

outputs._note: Phase 4A returns both raw and filtered numbers so the Pricing Agent (and Alex) can see what was excluded.
